title: lithocarpus cyrtorhynchus
intro: Lithocarpus cyrtorhynchus, also known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus or Himalayan Bur oak, is a species of plant in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and other mountainous regions of Asia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Lithocarpus cyrtorhynchus can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
